concerns.text: Tasks T029–T033 (Addressing Turing, Kandel, von Neumann, Pauling, Wolfram) have no corresponding Functional Requirement (FR) or User Story (US) in spec.md. The spec strictly defines the model via FR-004 (LIF neurons) and FR-005 (surrogate gradients). These tasks introduce unrequested architectural changes (e.g., 'slow plasticity', 'probabilistic thresholds') that are not traceable to any spec element, creating scope drift and potential implementation of non-mandated features.

Task T011 ('Implement statistical tests') is placed in the 'Foundational' phase, but the spec defines statistical analysis as the core of User Story 3 (FR-009, FR-010, FR-011). While implementation can happen early, the task description implies execution/validation before data exists. More critically, the spec requires 'paired t-tests' (FR-009), but the plan (Complexity Tracking) explicitly claims 'Unpaired Statistical Design' is required. The tasks do not resolve this contradiction; T011 and T024 must be aligned with the spec's mandatory 'paired' requirement, not the plan's contradictory 'unpaired' claim.

Tasks T029–T033 reference external figures (Alan Turing, Eric Kandel, etc.) that do not exist as IDs in the spec.md or plan.md. These appear to be stale tags or hallucinated requirements from an older iteration or external context not present in the current spec. They should be removed or mapped to actual spec requirements if they were intended to cover specific FRs.
